quick_facts.label: Conducting body and purpose
quick_facts.value: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) conducts this competitive recruitment examination for Multi-Tasking (Non-Technical) Staff, a General Central Service Group C, non-gazetted, non-ministerial post in central-government ministries, departments, offices, and specified statutory or constitutional bodies.

quick_facts.label: Minimum qualification
quick_facts.value: In the 2025 notice, applicants had to have passed Matriculation (Class 10) or an equivalent qualification from a recognized board by the stated cut-off date. Requirements must be checked again in the notice for the cycle being applied to.

quick_facts.label: Age context
quick_facts.value: For the 2025 cycle, the age band was 18–25 years for MTS, while an 18–27 band applied to Havaldar and to some MTS posts; category-based upper-age relaxations were listed in that notice. These dates and limits are cycle- and post-specific.

quick_facts.label: Exam structure
quick_facts.value: The 2025 scheme used a computer-based objective test in two mandatory 45-minute sessions: Numerical and Mathematical Ability; Reasoning Ability and Problem Solving; General Awareness; and English Language and Comprehension. Session I had no negative marking; Session II deducted one mark for each wrong answer.

quick_facts.label: 2026 schedule context
quick_facts.value: SSC’s 2026–27 tentative calendar listed the MTS and Havaldar 2026 advertisement for June 2026, closing in July 2026, and CBE in September–November 2026. A calendar is tentative; applicants should rely on the live SSC notice and application portal for final dates.

sections.heading: What the SSC MTS examination is for
paragraphs: SSC MTS is a national competitive recruitment route into Multi-Tasking (Non-Technical) Staff posts at Pay Level 1 under the 7th Central Pay Commission matrix. The 2025 SSC notice describes MTS as a General Central Service Group C, non-gazetted and non-ministerial post across central-government ministries, departments, offices, constitutional bodies, statutory bodies, and tribunals in States and Union Territories.

The same examination notice also included Havaldar recruitment in CBIC and CBN under the Department of Revenue. Havaldar is a separate post choice with additional physical testing, so candidates seeking only MTS should read the post-specific provisions in the applicable notice carefully.
bullets: The examination is a recruitment process, not a diploma or academic qualification.
The number, location, and category of vacancies are reported by user departments and may differ by recruitment cycle.
Pay and service conditions should be verified from the specific recruitment notice and appointing department; Pay Level 1 is the classification stated in the 2025 notice.

sections.heading: Eligibility: Class 10 is the educational entry point
paragraphs: The latest full official notice used here—the SSC MTS and Havaldar Examination, 2025 notice—required a pass in Matriculation or an equivalent examination from a recognized board by August 1, 2025. At document verification, candidates were required to produce original proof of completing Matriculation or its equivalent; an equivalence certificate is required where an equivalent qualification is claimed.

That notice set the MTS age range at 18–25 years as of August 1, 2025. It separately specified 18–27 years for Havaldar and for a few MTS posts in various departments, and it provided prescribed upper-age relaxations for eligible categories. Treat these as 2025-cycle examples rather than permanent limits: the next notification controls the applicable cut-off date, age bands, relaxations, nationality rules, disability suitability, and documents.
bullets: Confirm that the Class 10 result was declared by the notice’s cut-off date; merely having a result under processing did not meet the 2025 rule.
Keep the Class 10/secondary certificate, mark sheet or provisional certificate, and any equivalence or category documents ready for later verification.
Review the post-specific disability suitability and accommodation provisions in the live notification if applicable.

sections.heading: Test format and the Havaldar distinction
paragraphs: For MTS, the 2025 scheme consisted of a Computer Based Examination (CBE). It had two mandatory sessions on the same day, each lasting 45 minutes: Session I covered Numerical and Mathematical Ability and Reasoning Ability and Problem Solving, while Session II covered General Awareness and English Language and Comprehension. The paper used objective multiple-choice questions.

The 2025 CBE allocated 20 questions/60 marks to each Session I subject and 25 questions/75 marks to each Session II subject. There was no negative marking in Session I, but one mark was deducted for each incorrect Session II answer. SSC stated that Session I and the General Awareness component could be offered in English, Hindi, and 13 regional languages, while English Language and Comprehension necessarily assesses English.

Candidates applying for Havaldar faced an additional Physical Efficiency Test and Physical Standard Test after the CBE. In the 2025 notice, the PET walking standard was 1,600 metres in 15 minutes for men and 1 kilometre in 20 minutes for women; physical standards were also specified. Those additional requirements do not apply to ordinary MTS recruitment, but the current notice should always be checked.
bullets: Base preparation on the official four-subject syllabus, rather than assuming the pattern will remain identical every year.
Answer carefully in Session II because wrong answers carried a penalty under the 2025 rules.
If choosing Havaldar, begin physical preparation early and review the precise PET/PST and medical requirements in the applicable notice.

sections.heading: How to apply and track the recruitment cycle
paragraphs: Applications are made through SSC’s online system. SSC states that One-Time Registration (OTR) is a mandatory prerequisite before a candidate can apply for an examination. In the 2025 MTS process, candidates logged in with their registration credentials, selected the live MTS/Havaldar examination, supplied qualification and preference information, captured a photograph through the module, uploaded a signature, reviewed the form, submitted it, and made payment where applicable.

Application dates and fees vary by cycle. As dated context only, the 2025 notice prescribed a ₹100 application fee and exempted women candidates and eligible SC, ST, PwBD, and ex-servicemen candidates. SSC’s 2026–27 tentative calendar listed June 2026 for the MTS/Havaldar advertisement, July 2026 for closing, and September–November 2026 for the CBE; final dates must be taken from the current live notice, not from the calendar alone.

After applying, retain the submitted application and payment record, monitor the SSC site for city/admission-certificate information and notices, and bring the prescribed original identification and documents to examination or verification stages. SSC’s 2025 notice stated that application details are verified against original documents and that unsubstantiated claims can lead to cancellation of candidature.
bullets: Create or update SSC OTR before the application window closes.
Read the live notice before choosing post-cum-State/UT preferences and examination-centre preferences.
Check the final fee, exemptions, correction window, photograph/signature rules, and document checklist in the applicable cycle’s notice.
Use only SSC’s official website for notices, application access, answer keys, and results.

sections.heading: Preparation approach aligned to the official syllabus
paragraphs: Build a study plan around the CBE’s four areas. The 2025 syllabus identifies arithmetic topics such as integers, fractions, percentage, ratio and proportion, work and time, interest, profit and loss, geometry basics, distance and time, and simple graphs/data for Numerical and Mathematical Ability. Reasoning includes alpha-numeric series, coding-decoding, analogy, directions, similarities and differences, problem solving, diagrams, age calculations, calendars, and clocks.

For General Awareness, SSC’s 2025 indicative syllabus covered social studies—history, geography, art and culture, civics and economics—along with general science and environmental studies up to Class 10 level. English preparation should cover vocabulary, grammar, sentence structure, synonyms, antonyms, correct usage, and comprehension.

Practise the two-session structure under timed conditions. Since the 2025 pattern assigned different marking rules to the sessions, use a deliberate attempt strategy: prioritise accurate, well-practised work in both sessions and be especially selective in the negatively marked Session II. Use official SSC notices, syllabus material, answer keys, and prior papers where available; recheck the new notification for any changes before the exam.
bullets: Schedule separate timed practice for mathematics/reasoning and for general awareness/English.
Maintain an error log for arithmetic methods, reasoning patterns, grammar errors, and guessed Session II answers.
For Havaldar applicants, pair written-test preparation with regular walking and a review of the official PET/PST standards.

sections.heading: What selection can lead to
paragraphs: Successful candidates may be selected for MTS posts in central-government ministries, departments, offices, constitutional bodies, statutory bodies, and tribunals. The 2025 notice stated that selected MTS candidates could be posted in different States or Union Territories and should be willing to serve anywhere in India, subject to user-department requirements.

Selection is conditional rather than automatic upon taking or passing the CBE. SSC processes merit and allocates candidates against reported vacancies and stated preferences, while the relevant user department or organisation conducts document verification after the final result. Candidates must substantiate educational, category, age-relaxation, and other claims with the required originals.

For the 2025 recruitment, MTS was classified at Pay Level 1. Exact take-home pay, allowances, vacancy counts, posting location, duties, and joining conditions are variable and should be confirmed from the recruitment notice, allocation result, and appointing department at the time of selection.
bullets: MTS offers a direct Class-10-entry pathway to eligible central-government Group C support roles.

Candidates can continue education separately, including pursuing a polytechnic diploma if eligible under the admitting institution’s rules; SSC MTS itself is an employment examination, not a diploma pathway.
Havaldar outcomes are limited to the CBIC/CBN posts included in the relevant notice and require clearing the additional physical stages.
